THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: THEREFORE, IT IS HEREBY ADJUDGED AND DECREED THAT PLAINTIFF AJAY GUPTA RECOVER $58,654.14 FROM DEFENDANT IP&R INVENTORS' PUBLISHING & RESEARCH, LLC, AND GLOBAL 1 GROUP, INC., RECOVER $60,500 IN COMPENSATORY DAMAGES FROM DEFENDANT IP&R INVENTORS' PUBLISHING & RESEARCH, LLC. IT IS FURTHER ADJUDGED AND DECREED THAT PLAINTIFFS RECOVER $55,000 IN PUNITIVE DAMAGES, TOGETHER WITH COSTS OF SUIT. IT IS FURTHER HEREBY ADJUDGED AND DECREED THAT THE CROSS-COMPLAINANTS HEREBY TAKE NOTHING BY WAY OF THEIR CROSS-COMPLAINT. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

Off Calendar Mar-20-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to Defendant's Counsel Michael Carlson for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar/Attorney substituted out. Proceedings reported by: Patrick Heard, CSR#11055. (212)

Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Compel Ansers To Writtend Iscovery, Find Privileges Waived, Establish Admissions, & For Sanctions; Stmt Of Written Discovery In Dispute
Off Calendar Dec-20-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCE:
Peter M. Bransten, Esq., for defendant and moving party.
Ardell Johnson, Esq., and Joseph Wall, Jr., Esq., for plaintiff & opposing party.

AFTER HEARING, Defendant IP & R Investors' motion to compel responses to discovery, find privileges waived, and to establish admissions is granted in part and denied in part. Initially, the Court finds that defendant/cross-complainant has adequately identified the alleged trade secrets pursuant to CCP 2019.210. The Court also finds that plaintiff preserved their objections to the specified discovery during the meet and confer process as indicated in Exhibit C of the opposition as well the Notice of Objections to discovery served on September 13, 2007. Accordingly, the motion to find privileges waived and to establish admissions is denied. The motion to compel responses to discovery is granted. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to setting a schedule that priotizes when certain responses shall be served. Plaintiff shall complete the process of serving verified responses on or before January 21, 2008. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion From Waiver Of Objections
Off Calendar Dec-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE STATED BY EITHER PARTY. AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant IP & R Inventors' motion for relief from waiver of objections is granted. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to the responses. The Court notes that the general objections should be stricken as they are not authorized by the Code of Civil Procedure. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.
